subject: Rue Saint-Viateur, Mile End, Montreal, October 2018. Looking northeast between avenue du Parc and rue Jeanne-Mance. The original Saint-Viateur Bagel location is visible at the far left of the frame.
context_for_lesson: A 2018 photograph of rue Saint-Viateur, the heart of historic Jewish Montreal in the Mile End neighborhood. This is the area where many Hungarian Jewish refugees including Judy Abrams's family resettled after arriving in Canada in 1949. The Saint-Viateur Bagel shop visible at the left edge of the frame is one of the original Jewish-immigrant-founded businesses on the street. Pairs naturally with the 1948 Halifax Pier 21 immigrant photograph to form a Then-and-Now image-comparison slider for the Canadian arrival waypoint.
author: Andre Carrotflower
license_summary: CC BY-SA 4.0, free to use, share, and adapt with attribution; derivative works must use the same license
attribution_required: Photo: Andre Carrotflower via Wikimedia Commons (CC BY-SA 4.0)
lesson_use_intent: Use as the 'now' frame in a Then-and-Now image-comparison slider for the Mile End / Montreal waypoint. Pair with halifax-pier21-immigrant-woman-baby-1948.jpg as the 'then' frame. Caption should explain that Pier 21 Halifax was the arrival point and Mile End Montreal was the destination neighborhood for many Hungarian Jewish refugees including Judy's family.
